questions.question: Who among the following sent a telegram on 6 August 1857 stating, 'Our men are cowed by the numbers opposed to them and the endless fighting. Every village is held against us, the zamindars have risen to oppose us'?
In which of the following cities was the Paramhansa Mandali founded to work for the abolition of caste?
In which of the following years did the Government of Bengal pass the Calcutta Electric Lighting Act?
Which of the following transportation systems was primarily developed in India to serve colonial economic interests?
Which of the following subjects was NOT a part of the Transferred Subjects under the Government of India Act of 1919?
In the context of British plantations in India, what does the term ‘bigha’ refer to?
questions.option_1: A revenue estate which has one or more villages
questions.option_2: An agreement made with the ryots
questions.option_3: A plant that replaced indigo in Britain
questions.option_4: A unit of measurement of land

questions.question: Who among the following passed an Act in 1898, making it an offence to provoke people against the British rulers?
Who among the following strongly criticised British education for being too Western and disconnected from Indian realities?
Which organisation was established in 1817 to promote Western education among Indians?

He was a militant nationalist who believed that the only way to India’s freedom is through mass protests, especially by the youth. For this purpose, he began celebrating local festivals like Ganesh Chaturthi and Shivaji Jayanti on a grand scale, urging the youth to join in. He was also the editor of ‘Kesari’, a Marathi newspaper, which became one of the strongest critics of British rule. The leader in question is:

Raja Ram Mohan Roy was an active social reformer who spoke greatly for the problems faced by women. He was an inseparable agent who brought down the social evil of ‘sati’. The reform association founded by him was:
In which of the following years did Edward, Prince of Wales visit India on behalf of his father King George V, to thank the nation for the essential role it had played during the First World War?
Who introduced the Permanent Settlement in British India?
During the Revolt of 1857, Rani Laxmibai led the rebellion from:
Gandhiji gave a call for a satyagraha against the Rowlatt Act in which of the following years?
The Faraizi Movement was a religious reform movement of the 19ᵗʰ century in _______.
Which of the following was NOT a consequence of the Revolt of 1857?
questions.option_1: Lands and properties of Muslims were confiscated en masse.
questions.option_2: Policies that favoured zamindars and landlords were struck down.
questions.option_3: The strength of Indian soldiers in the army was reduced.
questions.option_4: The Governor-General of India was given the title 'Viceroy'.

questions.question: What does the term ‘satyagraha’ primarily mean in Gandhian philosophy for mass mobilisation and India’s struggle for freedom during colonial rule?
During the colonial period, the manufacturing sector accounted for only ______.
Who was the first Finance Minister of Independent India?
Which system of land tenure prevalent before independence led to exploitation of farmers?
Which of the following was the biggest armed resistance to colonialism in the 19ᵗʰ century in India?
Which reform movement in Kerala focused on the upliftment of the backward castes through education and social equality?
Land revenue systems under British rule varied regionally. Which land revenue system was primarily followed in Bombay and Madras Presidencies?
Who among the following laid the foundation stone of Indira Gandhi Rashtriya Uran Akademi, commonly called IGRUA, in 1985?
